documents.text: OF VETERANS AVERS VHA Medical Veterans Health Administration Documentation ENITED STATES OF AMERICA Document Created: 04/10/2026 17:51 The documents accompanying this transmission contain confidential health information that is legally privileged. This information is intended only for the use of the individual or entity providing care to this Veteran. The authorized recipient of this information is prohibited from disclosing this information to any other party unless required to do so by law or regulation and is required to destroy the information after its stated need has been fulfilled and in accordance with agency destruction and retention requirements. If you are not the intended recipient, you are hereby notified that any disclosure, copying, or distribution is strictly prohibited as this information is protected by Federal Privacy law (e.g., HIPAA Privacy Rule). If you have received this information in error, please notify the sender immediately and arrange for the return or destruction of these documents. LAS VEGAS HCS 6900 North Pecos Road, Las Vegas, Nevada 89086-4400 VA Referrals phone: 702-791-9066 Fax: 702-791-9217 Point of Contact (POC): Batara, Llowielyn POC Phone: Fax: Patient Name: BAIER,JAMES L DOB: 11/23/1940 59 PANGLOSS ST Phone (residential): (702) 972-3231 Referral Type: Community Care HENDERSON, NEVADA 89002 Phone (mobile): (725) 242-2397 Network Next of Kin contact information Address (Next Of Kin) BAIER,MARILYN 59 PANGLOSS Phone: (702)567-0550 HENDERSON, NEVADA 89002 CONSULTS Current PC Provider: HAMBY,KRYSTA A Current PC Team: SEC PACT 15 NP *WH* Current Pat. Status: Outpatient UCID: 593_6704081 Primary Eligibility: NSC(VERIFIED) Patient Type: NSC VETERAN OEF/OIF: NO Order Information To Service: COMMUNITY CARE-GEC SKILLED HOME CARE From Service: SEC PACT 15 NP Requesting Provider: HAMBY,KRYSTA A Service is to be rendered on an OUTPATIENT basis Place: Consultant's choice Urgency: Routine Clinically Ind. Date: Apr 10, 2026 DST ID: Orderable Item: COMMUNITY CARE-GEC SKILLED HOME CARE Consult: Consult Request Provisional Diagnosis: Venous Insufficiency (Chronic) (Peripheral)(ICD-10-CM 187.2) Reason For Request: Type of Service: Evaluation and Treatment Chief Complaint: Chronic venous insufficiency with resolving cellulitus Patient History / Clinical Findings / Diagnosis (Co-Morbidities): Chronic venous insufficiency, cellulitus, DM Contact Information for provider who will manage the home health episode of care: Provider name: krysta hamby, APRN Phone number: Fax number: The Veteran IS being referred under VA Pay SKILLED NURSING ORDERS Medication Supervision/Instruction Wound Care: Inter-facility Information This is not an inter-facility consult request. Status: ACTIVE Last Action: ADDED COMMENT Facility Activity Date/Time/Zone Responsible Person Entered By CPRS RELEASED ORDER 04/10/26 10:22 HAMBY, KRYSTA A HAMBY,KRYSTA A RECEIVED 04/10/26 10:47 BATARA,LLOWIELYN BATARA,LLOWIELYN CCE-CC Eligibility Status: ELIGIBLE VCC-Veteran CC option: OPT-IN BVP-Basis for Veteran Preference: Scheduling flexibility CSC-Consult stop code: -58 CSN-Clinical service: Skilled Home Health Care CST-Consult service type: Specialty Care SEV-CC Eligibility: Specific clinical service not available at the VA CCE ICR-Initiate Community Care Referral CAP-CC approved: Non-institutional Alternatives to Nursing Home Care - 1720C CCP Code 39 VSP-Veteran scheduling preference: Community provider schedules PPP-Veteran expressed preference for specific community provider(s): No SLD-Scheduling location discussed with Veteran: Community Care SAL-Selected appointment location: Community Care SEOC VHA Office of Community Care VHA Office of Community Care - Standardized Episode of Care Skilled Home Health Care Bundled, 120 Days CAT-SEOC CoC: SKILLED HOME HEALTH CARE SEOC ID: NIC_SKILLED HOME HEALTH CARE BUNDLED, 120 DAYS_1.6.4_REV Description: This authorization covers services associated with the specialty(s) identified for this episode of care, including all medical care listed below relevant to the referred care specified on the consult/referral order. Duration: 120 days Procedural Overview: NOTE: Apply this Standardized Episode of Care (SEOC) when bundled care using the (Outcome and Assessment Information Set (OASIS)/Health Insurance Prospective Payment System (HIPPS)) is applied. Bundled care refers to short-term acute intermittent in-home skilled care that would otherwise be covered by Medicare. NOTE: Patients do not need to meet Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services (CMS) home bound criteria and face to face requirements for these services. NOTE: Frequency of visits as clinically necessary and determined by treatment plan. NOTE: OASIS and treatment plan to occur per CMS regulations. NOTE: For this Bundled SEOC, claims for rendered services should be submitted with corresponding HIPPS codes (e.g., HAEJ1) NOTE: It is expected that a clinical review be completed and documented every 60 days to confirm care is appropriate for another certification period. 1. All home health services included in bundle listed below: 1A. Comprehensive (OASIS) evaluation for formulation of a treatment plan, to include comprehensive assessment and medication reconciliation. 1B. In-home skilled services, as clinically necessary, to potentially include the following disciplines: nursing, physical therapy, occupational therapy, speech therapy, nutritional services, social work services, home health aide, mental health and palliative skilled services. 2. Lab Processing when VA Lab location not readily available * Please visit the VHA Storefront (https://www.va.gov/COMMUNITYCARE/providers/index.asp) for additional resources pertaining to the following: * Pharmacy prescribing requirements and information for outpatient prescriptions * Durable Medical Equipment (DME), Prosthetics, and Orthotics prescribing requirements and information * Clinical Determinations and Indications (CDIs) requirements and information * Request for Services (RFS) requirements and information * Precertification requirements and information. This authorization is limited by precertification requirements. Services authorized on this referral may require precertification, which must be obtained prior to rendering services. Claims for precertification-required services will be denied if a precertification ID was not obtained prior to the date of service. Providers should routinely visit the Community Care Precertification Storefront Page (https://www.va.gov/COMMUNITYCARE/providers/PRCT-requirements.asp)to determine which services require precertification and to obtain precertification for those services. SEO RPR-Consult is not related to a previous RFS GDR-GEC DOA Review: Service is clinically appropriate GAP-CC approved: Home Health Care-1720C GRM-GEC referral will be managed using HSRM: Yes VCA-Is a VCA being used for this referral? NO RNO-Referral Number: VA0058192267 CUR-CTB User Role: Registered Nurse (RN)/Other Clinical Staff COM-Additional Comments: A PLUS HOME HEALTH SYSTEMS 5670 W Flamingo Rd Las Vegas, NV 89103 Ph: 702-720-7929 Fax: 702-342-8398 NPI: 1154824985 ENDORSED TO: JESSICA COM ADDED COMMENT 04/10/26 10:48 BATARA,LLOWIELYN BATARA,LLOWIELYN Above listed agency approved to evaluate and provide SKILLED NURSING for admission assessment, home safety evaluation, medication review and assessment of medication compliance, disease process/management teaching, patient/caregiver education reinforcement, vital signs check, as medically necessary. Skilled visits per each discipline's plan of care not to exceed SEOC authorization. Agencies must discharge sooner if goals are met, or when veteran has reached maximum potential. Veterans on VA pay do not need to meet Medicare homebound and face-to-face criteria. Agencies must be Medicare certified, Medicaid certified, or licensed by the state. Agencies to EMAIL the 485 care plans or orders to be signed to vhalaspurchaseskilledadmin@va.gov PCP/ordering Provider: KRYSTA HAMBY APRN Ph: 702-791-9030 Fax: FOR NON-URGENT/FYI NOTES ONLY: 702-856-1694 PLEASE EMAIL 485s/ORDERS TO BE SIGNED TO: vhalaspurchaseskilledadmin@va.gov For Medication or Supply Refills: Pharmacy 833-983-0487, PRESS 1 For Medical Assistance/Triage: 833-983-0487, PRESS 3 To Make an Appointment/Send message to Provider: 833-983-0487, PRESS 2 Please report INR results to AC PharmD: 702-791-9000 ext 13680 DME REQUESTS: - PT to fill out and sign RFS form - attach PT evaluation notes - Education box needs to be checked - indicate height and weight - Fax to VA DME coordinators 702-791-9224 PLEASE ENCOURAGE ALL VETERANS TO SIGN UP FOR MY HEALTH eVET My HealtheVet Office: VA Medical Center Building 1, 1C203 Phone: (702) 791-9000 Ext. 15436 **** AGENCIES MUST DISCHARGE VETERAN WHEN GOALS MET OR NO FURTHER NEED FOR SKILLED SERVICES**** VA Approval Form is available in the HSRM portal. Service dates TBD once SOC date reported. Access HSRM for the admission packet/other documents. Note: TIME ZONE is local if not indicated No local TIU results or Medicine results available for this consult END PROBLEM LIST Sensitive Diagnoses No sensitive diagnoses were provided. Signature Information Signed Date: APR 10, 2026@10:12:50 Signed By: GUERRERO, CHRISTIAN G Signature Mode: ELECTRONIC Cosigned Date: None Cosigned By: None Cosignature Mode: None Document Body PACT Nursing Intake Note Veteran confirmed identity by stating: Full Name and last four of social security number Reason for visit/appointment: Reason for visit: routine visit Vital Signs: Temperature: 98.7 F [37.1 C] (04/10/2026 09:58) Pulse: 100 (04/10/2026 09:58) Respirations: 16 (04/10/2026 09:58) Blood Pressure: 119/87 (04/10/2026 09:58) O2 Saturation: 97% (04/10/2026 09:58) Height: 63 in [160.0 cm] (04/10/2026 09:58) Weight: 235 lb [106.59 kg] (04/10/2026 09:58) PAIN: Are you currently experiencing pain? Yes - DVPRS scale used to assess Location: legs Defense and Veterans Pain Rating Scale (DVPRS): 3 Sometimes distracts me Pain Score: 4 Patient's acceptable pain goal: 0 No pain Primary Pain Assessment: Pain Type: Chronic Describe Pain (Quality): Musculoskeletal: Aching, Dull, Sharp Neurological: Tingling Pain Interventions: Positioning Other: tylenol Pain treatment: Yes Medication Review Patient/Veteran denies changes to medications. Fall risk Have you fallen in the past year? No FRAIL ELDERLY ASSESSMENT Assessment and Care of Vulnerable Elders (ACOVE): FALL HISTORY *Required Within the past 12 months, has the patient had any falls? Patient has not had any falls in the past 12 months. Home Hazard Screening Completed (includes: home layout, steps, rugs, handrails and lighting) No hazards noted per patient. Patient/family member has been instructed on individualized fall prevention measures, appropriate to their situation, according to guidance in the facility fall prevention program and has restated the information correctly. KATZ ADL FUNCTIONAL ASSESSMENT *Required Complete Index of ADL: ADL Scale: 18 A high index indicates independence. A low index means dependence on others to help with activities of daily living. 1. Bathing: either sponge bath, tub bath or shower. Receives no assistance (gets in and out of tub by self, if tub is usual means of bathing). 2. Dressing: gets clothes from closets and drawers, including under-clothes, outer garments and using fasteners (including braces if worn). Gets clothes and dresses self without assistance. 3. Toileting: going to the "toilet room" for bowel and urine elimination; cleaning self after elimination and arranging clothes. (May use cane, walker, or wheelchair, and manage bedpan or commode, emptying same next morning). No assistance needed. 4. Transfer: Moves in and out of bed, or chair, without assistance (may use support object like cane or walker). 5. Continence: Controls urination and bowel movement completely by self. 6. Feeding: Feeds self without assistance. LAWTON IADL FUNCTIONAL ASSESSMENT *Required Complete Index of IADL: IADL Scale: 8 A low score indicates dependence on others to help with daily activities. A high score indicates independent living. Range is 0 to 8. 1. Ability to use telephone Operates telephone on own initiative; looks up and dials numbers, etc. 2. Shopping Takes care of all shopping needs independently. 3. Food Preparation Plans, prepares and serves adequate meals independently. 4. Housekeeping Maintains house alone or with occasional assistance (e.g. heavy work domestic help). 5. Laundry Does personal laundry completely. 6. Mode of Transportation Travels independently on public transportation or drives own car. 7. Responsibility for own medications Is responsible for taking medication in correct dosages at correct time. 8. Ability to Handle Finances Manages financial matters independently (budgets, writes checks, goes to bank). URINARY INCONTINENCE SCREEN *Required Has the patient experienced urinary incontinence in the past 12 months? Patient has not experience urinary incontinence in the past 12 months. Provider notified: Hamby, Krysta NP Abuse Screen ABUSE SCREEN REMINDER Do you feel safe at home physically, emotionally, and financially? Yes Stress Assessment (Nursing should document the patients response to the following question): How is your stress level today? "My life is not perfect but not the worst, I have some stress but I can handle it" Additional Interventions: OBJECTIVE: 85 y/o male Alert and Oriented 1. Time Yes 2. Person Yes 3. Place Yes Plan: To see provider today Whole Health program discussed &/or education handout provided Education provided today? No Clinical Reminders: Alcohol Use Screen (AUDIT-C): Alcohol Screen: SCREEN FOR ALCOHOL (AUDIT-C) An alcohol screening test (AUDIT-C) was negative (score=0). 1. How often did you have a drink containing alcohol in the past year? Consider a drink to be a 12 ounce can or bottle of regular beer, 8 ounces of malt liquor, a 5 ounce glass of table wine, or a 1.5 ounce shot of liquor (like scotch, gin, or vodka). Never 2. How many drinks containing alcohol did you have on a typical day when you were drinking in the past year? Response not required due to responses to other questions. 3. How often did you have six or more drinks on one occasion in the past year? Response not required due to responses to other questions. RSV Immunization: Respiratory Syncytial Virus (RSV) Vaccine: Phone/ Virtual/Telehealth Visit - Patient educated on the need for receiving Respiratory Syncytial Virus vaccine either at VA or outside facility. Pneumococcal Vaccine: ++ This Veteran has a past pneumococcal vaccination that is recorded without a formulation/type specified. In order to determine the correct vaccine, prior pneumococcal vaccine(s) must be reviewed and entered correctly. If prior vaccination records cannot be confirmed, the Veteran may be considered to have unknown vaccination status and provider should determine whether revaccination is indicated. ++ Telephone/Virtual/Telehealth Visit - Patient educated on the need for receiving Pneumococcal vaccine either at VA or outside facility. Provider should always submit the RFS directly to the authorizing VAMC, preferably via the HSRM portal. Providers should always submit an RFS on the same day it is determined it's needed and before delivering care, unless it is emergent care. In that case, the RFS can be submitted simultaneously. How to Submit an RFS VA prefers providers submit an RFS via the HSRM portal, available on the VA's website Go to the VA Storefront at https://ww.va.gov/COMMUNITYCARE/providers/Care-Cordinatin.asp#RFS Navigate to the link to the RFS form at the bottom of the section Medical Records and Documents Requirements Medical Records and documentation are required for all provider services. Providers are required to submit Medical documentation directly to the authorizing VAMC, preferably via upload to HSRM. Clinical Determinations and Indications VA Clinical Determinations and Indications (CDIs) describe standard VA health care benefits for services and procedures that community providers may recommend as necessary for a Veteran. Prior to providing care, providers should use the CDIs as a reference when determining if a Veteran meets VA clinical criteria. When additional services are requested, www.va.gov/COMMUNITYCARE/providers/info-CDI.asp CDIs will be used to determine approval by a clinical reviewer. CDIs and supporting information can be found at: https:// Medical Devices As part of furnishing the services authorized on the approved referral, you may recommend that medical devices, adaptive equipment, or other items be provided for the treatment or rehabilitation of the Veteran's medical condition. In such instances, you must complete a Request for Services (RFS) as outlined above. https://www.va.gov/COMMUNITYCARE/providers/Care-Cordination.asp#RFS The RFS must (1) indicate that the condition for which the item is being prescribed is within the scope of the authorized services on the approved referral, (2) describe the item or service being prescribed with as much specificity as possible (including manufacturer and model, needed custom measurements, size), and (3) provide a brief but thorough plain- language explanation of how the item or service will serve the treatment or rehabilitation needs of the Veteran. All parts of the RFS form must be filled out completely. VA staff will determine whether the prescribed item or service is one that VA is authorized to purchase. If the item is not something that we are authorized to purchase, VA staff will work with you to identify an alternative that will best meet the Veteran's clinical needs. In making this determination, VA staff will consider your clinical judgment and expertise and work with you to resolve outstanding issues quickly. In the event of an urgent or emergent need, you may provide medical devices, necessary items or services prescribed to Veterans receiving care in the community for urgent or emergent conditions at the time of healthcare service delivery or soon thereafter. Urgent or emergent DME or Medical Devices may include, but are not limited to splints, crutches, canes, slings, soft collars, walkers, and manual wheelchairs. DME dispensed directly to the Veteran based on an urgent or emergent need will be paid by the CCN Third Party Administrator. Claims for these items should be submitted using the process outlined in the Submitting Claims section of this form. CVS Caremark is the retail pharmacy network for Veterans' immediately needed or Urgent/Emergent Immediate need prescriptions: Must follow the VA Urgent/Emergent Formulary which can be found at http://www.pbm.va.gov/PBM/nationalformulary.asp Prescription can only go up to a 14-day supply. No refills of the immediate need medication may be authorized. Only a seven-day supply for opioids, or up to the opioid prescribing limit allowed by State-whichever is less-may be authorized. Immediate need prescription extending past 14 days: The provider will need to send second prescription (beyond 14 days) to the referring VA medical facility's pharmacy for prescription fulfillment services. Routine/maintenance prescriptions: Must be sent to the referring VA medical facility's pharmacy Prescriptions needed for care within a VA paid Skilled Nursing Facility (SNF) or Community Nursing Home (CNH) are to be provided by the SNF/CNH. If you do not have the ability to electronically submit prescriptions to pharmacies, please contact the Community Care representative at the referring VA medical facility for their pharmacy fax number. Please refer to: https://ww.a.gov/COMMUNITYCARE/providers/Senvice-Requirements.asp for additional instructions related to prescriptions.
